chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ طَلَّقَ ثَلَاثاً عَلَى طُهْرٍ بِشُهُودٍ فِي مَجْلِسٍ أَوْ أَكْثَرَ إِنَّهَا وَاحِدَةٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 10 – The one who divorces three (divorces) upon purity, with witnesses, in one sitting, or more, it is one (divorce)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/119)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/119)

parts.text: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَبُو الْعَبَّاسِ الرَّزَّازُ

text: الطَّلَاقُ ثَلَاثاً فِي غَيْرِ عِدَّةٍ إِنْ كَانَتْ عَلَى طُهْرٍ فَوَاحِدَةٌ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَى طُهْرٍ فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, and Muhammad Bin Ja’far Abu Al Abbas Al Razzaz, from Ayoub Bin Nuh, both together, from Safwan, from Mansour Bin Hazim, from Abu Baseer Al Asady, and Muhammad Bin Ali Al Halby, and Umar Bin Al Hanzala,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The three divorces without a waiting period, even if it was upon purity, so it is one, and if it did not happen upon purity, so it is nothing’.
en.sarwar: 3. Abu Ali al-Ash‘ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar and Muhammad ibn Ja‘far from abu al-‘Abbas al-Razzaz from abu Ayyub ibn Nuh all from Safwan from Mansur ibn Hazim from abu Basir al-Asadiy and Muhammad ibn Ali al-Halabiy and ‘Umar ibn Hanzalah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Divorce three times in non-waiting period (without waiting period) if she is clean from Hayd (menses) counts as one, but if she is not clean of Hayd (menses) then it (divorce) does not have any valid effect.’”
